notes: Confirmed: SkiaSharp does not natively support Netpbm (PPM/PGM/PBM) image formats. SKImage.FromEncodedData, SKCodec.Create, and SKBitmap.Decode all return null when given PPM files (both P3 ASCII and P6 binary). This is expected — Skia's built-in codecs cover PNG, JPEG, WebP, GIF, BMP, ICO, and WBMP, but not Netpbm. However, 24-bit RGB pixel data can be loaded manually using SKColorType.Rgb888x with InstallPixels, SetPixel, or by expanding to RGBA and using SKImage.FromPixelCopy. A community member provided a complete working solution in the issue comments.

reproductionSteps.description: Build and run repro — verify PPM native decode returns null and manual pixel loading works

--- Test 3: SKImage.FromEncodedData with P3 (ASCII) PPM ---
RESULT: SKImage.FromEncodedData returned null for ASCII PPM file

--- Test 4: SKBitmap.Decode with P6 PPM ---
RESULT: SKBitmap.Decode returned null for PPM file

--- Test 5: Manual pixel loading with Rgb888x ---
Pixel (0,0): R=255, G=0, B=0 (expected: Red)
SUCCESS: Manual Rgb888x pixel loading works

--- Test 6: SetPixel per-pixel approach ---
SUCCESS: SetPixel approach works

--- Test 7: SKImage.FromPixelCopy with Rgba8888 ---
SUCCESS: Created image from pixel copy: 4x2

SUCCESS: test completed

versionResults.notes: PPM not natively decodable. SKImage.FromEncodedData, SKCodec.Create, SKBitmap.Decode all return null. Manual pixel loading via Rgb888x works.
Same behavior as 2.88.9 — PPM not supported natively, manual pixel loading works.
Main branch source — same behavior. PPM codec not available in Skia built-in codecs.

actionability.reason: PPM is not a standard image format supported by Skia codec infrastructure. SkiaSharp provides raw pixel APIs (Rgb888x, InstallPixels, FromPixelCopy) that allow loading 24-bit RGB data. A community member already provided a complete working solution. This is a how-to question, not a missing feature.

actions.description: Close as answered — question resolved by community solution and confirmed by reproduction

workarounds: Parse PPM header manually, create SKBitmap with SKColorType.Rgb888x and SKAlphaType.Opaque, then use SetPixel to load pixels one-by-one
Convert 3-byte RGB to 4-byte RGBX, pin with GCHandle, and use SKBitmap.InstallPixels for better performance
Expand 3-byte RGB to 4-byte RGBA (insert 0xFF alpha), then use SKImage.FromPixelCopy with SKColorType.Rgba8888
